examples.title: Solving Ticket Prices with Unitary Method
examples.content:
If **$8$ concert tickets** cost **$96$ dollars**, how much will **$5$ concert tickets** cost?

### Step-by-Step Solution

1.  **Step 1: Find the value of a single unit (1 ticket)**:
    Divide the total cost by the number of tickets:
    $$\text{Cost of 1 ticket} = \frac{96 \text{ dollars}}{8 \text{ tickets}} = 12 \text{ dollars per ticket}$$

2.  **Step 2: Scale up to the target quantity (5 tickets)**:
    Multiply the cost of a single ticket by $5$:
    $$\text{Cost of 5 tickets} = 12 \text{ dollars/ticket} \times 5 = 60 \text{ dollars}$$

**Answer**: **$60$ dollars**.

examples.title: Scaling Rates: Plant Growth over Time
examples.content:
A bamboo plant grows **$18\text{ cm}$** over a period of **$6\text{ weeks}$** at a perfectly steady, constant rate. How much will it grow in **$10\text{ weeks}$**?

### Step-by-Step Solution

1.  **Step 1: Find the unit growth rate (growth in 1 week)**:
    Divide the total growth by the number of weeks:
    $$\text{Growth in 1 week} = \frac{18 \text{ cm}}{6 \text{ weeks}} = 3 \text{ cm per week}$$

2.  **Step 2: Multiply by the target number of weeks ($10$)**:
    Multiply the weekly growth rate by the new duration of $10$ weeks:
    $$\text{Growth in 10 weeks} = 3 \text{ cm/week} \times 10 \text{ weeks} = 30 \text{ cm}$$

**Answer**: The plant will grow **$30\text{ cm}$**.
